name: HarmonE
description: A self-adaptive approach to architecting sustainable MLOps. HarmonE monitors accuracy, energy use, and data shifts to keep ML pipelines running efficiently while maintaining performance.
longDescription: HarmonE is an architectural approach that helps machine learning pipelines adapt on their own. It monitors accuracy, energy use, and data changes to keep things running smoothly. It only takes action when needed, based on sustainability goals set in advance. HarmonE uses a MAPE-K loop (Monitor, Analyze, Plan, Execute, Knowledge) to drive adaptation and has been tested on city traffic systems, showing 54.5% less energy usage than traditional retraining approaches while achieving 95% of the best model's accuracy.

name: Harmonica
description: A self-adaptation exemplar for sustainable MLOps that operationalizes the HarmonE approach with a runnable implementation.
longDescription: Harmonica is a fully runnable tool designed to enable the sustainable operation of MLOps pipelines. It demonstrates how a structured MAPE-K loop can be integrated into real MLOps pipelines to support sustainability, long-term viability, and runtime resilience in Machine-Learning-enabled Systems (MLS). The tool includes a managing system that oversees the MLS at runtime and enforces adaptation policies decoupled from system execution, featuring a web-based dashboard for visualization and policy management.

name: EnCoDe
description: A methodology for fine-grained, design-time energy estimation of source code. EnCoDe predicts energy consumption of individual code blocks—functions, loops, and conditionals—without executing them, using static code features and ML models.
longDescription: EnCoDe (Energy Estimation of Source Code At Design-Time) addresses the gap left by runtime-only profiling tools by enabling developers to estimate energy consumption at design time. It introduces PowerLens, a novel sub-millisecond measurement methodology that reliably captures energy readings for microsecond-scale code blocks using execution amplification, RAPL temporal synchronization, and IQR-based aggregation. An empirical study on over 18,000 Python programs yields a dataset of executable code blocks annotated with 33 static AST features across 7 categories. XGBoost models trained on these features achieve R² = 0.755 for continuous energy regression and 80.6% accuracy for Low/Medium/High tier classification—without running any code. EnCoDe is operationalized as WattWise, a VS Code extension that surfaces inline energy estimates, AI-powered optimization suggestions, a repo-wide energy dashboard, and a GitHub PR bot for catching energy regressions.
